ogTitle: Worlds End vs Wrattonbully: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Worlds End and Wrattonbully.
heading: Worlds End vs Wrattonbully — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Worlds End, South Australia and Wrattonbully, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wrattonbully has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Worlds End (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Worlds End or Wrattonbully safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Worlds End has a flood risk score of 49/100, while Wrattonbully scores 27/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Worlds End scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Wrattonbully scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Worlds End or Wrattonbully?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wrattonbully has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
